problem: Background invoice jobs stopped running after a scheduler change.
summary: Invoice generation halted because the scheduler migration silently dropped the invoice job definitions and no liveness monitoring existed for batch jobs; both conditions had to hold for the outage to persist unnoticed.

why_chain.question: Why did invoice jobs stop running?
why_chain.answer: The new scheduler had no registered invoice job definitions after the migration.

why_chain.question: Why were the job definitions missing?
why_chain.answer: The migration script only converted jobs listed in the primary config file, and invoice jobs lived in a legacy include file.

why_chain.question: Why did nobody notice for days?
why_chain.answer: There is no liveness alert on batch job completion, so silence looked like success.

why_chain.question: Why was there no liveness monitoring?
why_chain.answer: Batch observability was never made a requirement of the job platform; monitoring exists only for request-serving services.

root_cause: The scheduler migration process had no completeness verification for migrated job definitions, compounded by absent batch-job liveness monitoring.
contributing_factors: Migration was verified by spot-checking a subset of jobs.
No owner is assigned for batch observability.
recommendations: Add a post-migration reconciliation step that diffs job inventories between old and new schedulers.
Add liveness alerts keyed on expected completion windows for every production batch job.
Consolidate legacy include-file job definitions into the primary configuration.
assumptions: The legacy include file was the only location of the dropped job definitions.
evidence_needed: Job inventory exports from the old and new schedulers.
Scheduler migration script logs from the cutover window.

children: Invoice job definitions missing from new scheduler
No liveness alert on batch completion

gates.event: Invoice job definitions missing from new scheduler
children: Migration script ignored legacy include file

basic_causes: Migration lacked a completeness reconciliation step
Batch observability never made a platform requirement
